title: Velvety Sweet Uruguayan Pancetta Skillet
description: A tangy and pillowy Uruguayan dish featuring pancetta, perfect for a date night, cooked in a stone bowl dolsot and friendly for those with nut allergies.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Cook the pancetta slices in a large skillet over medium heat until crispy.
Remove the pancetta from the skillet and set aside.
Add the sliced onion to the skillet and cook until caramelized.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for 1 minute.
Add the diced red bell pepper to the skillet and cook until tender.
Add the halved cherry tomatoes to the skillet and cook until they start to release their juices.
Create 4 wells in the skillet and crack an egg into each well.
Sprinkle shredded cheese over the eggs and cook until the eggs are set.
Return the cooked pancetta to the skillet and stir to combine.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 10 minutes.
